title: philotheca nodiflora subsp. lasiocalyx
intro: Philotheca nodiflora subsp. lasiocalyx, also known as the Westringia or coastal rosemary, is a flowering plant species native to Western Australia. The medicinal properties of this plant include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Philotheca nodiflora subsp. lasiocalyx include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and aid in digestion, as well as its potential to boost the immune system and relieve stress. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
